decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with low-cost aggressive creatures and spells that can deal damage early in the game. The key strategy is to constantly put pressure on the opponent, utilizing cards that can grow stronger with each spell cast and efficiently clear the opponent's board. By maintaining a fast tempo and constantly pushing damage through, the deck aims to win the game before the opponent can stabilize. Utilizing a mix of cheap creatures and direct damage spells, this deck aims to end the game quickly and decisively.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing powerful removal spells, hand disruption, and creatures that can disrupt the opponent's strategy. Mono Black Control aims to outvalue the opponent by utilizing cards like Thoughtseize, Fatal Push, and Liliana of the Veil to control the board state. Additionally, the deck includes win conditions like Morlun, Devourer of Spiders and Sheoldred, the Apocalypse to close out the game. The deck also includes utility lands like Castle Locthwain and Field of Ruin to ensure consistency and flexibility in gameplay.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively control the board and disrupt your opponent's game plan while quickly building up your own threats. Utilizing powerful control spells like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push, alongside versatile creatures like Spyglass Siren and Moon-Circuit Hacker, you aim to quickly overwhelm your opponent. The inclusion of cards like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Bitter Triumph add additional power and resilience to your board presence. Overall, this deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain control throughout the game to secure a swift victory.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly play low-cost spells and creatures to pressure the opponent and deal damage efficiently. The deck includes a mix of burn spells, card draw, and aggressive creatures to maintain a fast pace and outpace the opponent. The synergy between the cheap spells and creatures allows for quick damage output and strong board presence. Additionally, the inclusion of counter spells provides some control over the opponent's plays, while the land base ensures consistent access to the necessary mana for casting spells.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on using cards that disrupt the opponent's strategy while gaining card advantage with Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion. The deck includes a variety of removal spells, counterspells, and cards that bounce or exile threats. The mana base includes lands that can produce both blue and black mana, as well as utility lands for added flexibility. The deck aims to control the game by consistently dealing with threats and outvaluing opponents through card advantage.

This deck focuses on combining the abilities of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. The deck includes cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the opponent's board and disrupt their strategy. Monument to Endurance and Iron-Shield Elf provide additional support to protect the key combo pieces. The deck also includes various lands to ensure mana consistency and utility. The overall strategy is to quickly assemble the combo pieces and launch a devastating offensive against the opponent.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly play aggressive creatures and spells to deal damage to the opponent as fast as possible. The key strategy is to use cheap and efficient spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to clear the way for creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear to attack. The deck also utilizes card draw with Sleight of Hand and Academic Dispute to maintain momentum and pressure. By maintaining a fast pace and keeping up the pressure, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish a strong defense.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on dealing damage quickly and efficiently through a mix of burn spells and low-cost creatures with prowess abilities. The goal is to control the board and apply pressure early, utilizing spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to remove threats while creatures like Soul-Scar Mage and Monastery Swiftspear chip away at the opponent's life total. With a focus on tempo and aggression, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can establish their own game plan.

This aggro deck aims to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive red creatures and burn spells. The key strategy is to apply constant pressure on the opponent's life total, utilizing efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Bonecrusher Giant, along with burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage. The inclusion of utility lands like Den of the Bugbear and Ramunap Ruins provides additional reach and versatility in the late game. Overall, the deck focuses on speedy aggression and direct damage to secure a quick victory.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang and Parhelion II combo to generate a powerful board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes cards like Thoughtseize and Cache Grab to disrupt the opponent's strategy while also deploying threats like Esika's Chariot and Overlord of the Balemurk to apply pressure. With a mix of card draw, removal, and powerful creatures, this deck aims to control the game until it can assemble its key combo pieces and secure victory.

The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan through hand disruption and removal spells, while also utilizing powerful creatures and enchantments to take control of the board. It aims to outvalue the opponent in the mid to late game by leveraging card advantage and efficient removal options. The deck's strategy is to grind out incremental advantages until it can establish a dominant position and close out the game with its powerful threats.

This deck focuses on utilizing a combination of aggressive creatures and spells to put pressure on the opponent and control the board. It includes a mix of efficient creatures and powerful spells to maintain tempo and momentum in the game. The deck aims to quickly establish a board presence and deal damage to the opponent steadily, while also being able to control threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan. It offers a versatile playstyle that can adapt to different matchups and provides a combination of aggro and control elements to outmaneuver the opponent.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with instants and sorceries to bring back Arclight Phoenix and apply pressure. It includes cards that can draw and filter through the deck efficiently, as well as removal spells to deal with opposing threats. The key strategy is to cast multiple spells in a turn to trigger the Phoenix's ability and return it to the battlefield, overwhelming the opponent with a flurry of attacks. The deck aims to maintain a fast pace and outmaneuver opponents through card advantage and powerful recurring threats.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan while generating value through efficient removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures. By using cards like Duress and Thoughtseize to strip key cards from the opponent's hand, along with removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to handle threats on the board, the deck aims to grind out advantages over time. The inclusion of powerful finishers like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Graveyard Trespasser also allows the deck to close out games once it has gained control.

The main concept of this Dimir Control deck is to control the game through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. By using cards like Narset, Parter of Veils and Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while utilizing cards like Fatal Push and Boomerang Basics to remove threats, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional value and card advantage in longer games. Ultimately, the goal is to maintain control of the game until victory is achieved.

This UR aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, backed up by a mix of burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage. The deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent by efficiently clearing the way for its aggressive creatures and finishing the game before the opponent can stabilize. In addition, the deck utilizes cantrips like Sleight of Hand and Flow State to dig for key pieces and maintain a steady stream of threats. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive game plan with a mix of lands that provide both red and blue mana sources.
